rxn_class_summary: Copper-catalyzed three-component asymmetric radical 1,2-carboalkynylation of alkenes with terminal alkynes and alkyl halides using a chiral cinchona-derived N,N,P-ligand to deliver chiral alkynes

key_intermediates.name: L1Cu(II)-acetylide (III) generated after SET to alkyl halide
benzylic/prochiral alkyl radical IV stabilized by delocalization into the (hetero)aromatic ring (after addition to alkene)

electron_flow: L1Cu(I)-acetylide (II) reduces alkyl halide R2-X by inner-sphere SET to generate an alkyl radical ·R2 and L1Cu(II)-acetylide (III); ·R2 adds to the terminal alkene to give a benzylic radical IV, which combines with Cu(II) species III to form Cu(III) intermediate VI that reductively eliminates the C(sp3)-C(sp) bond, regenerating L1Cu(I).
rate_determining_step: C(sp3)-C(sp) bond-forming reductive elimination from Cu(III) intermediate VI is proposed as the enantiodetermining step; kinetic orders (first order in alkene, catalyst, and alkyl halide; saturation in alkyne) are consistent with turnover-limiting steps involving alkene, catalyst, and R-X but not free alkyne

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction inhibited, supporting a radical pathway

probe_reagents.name: radical clock substrate 82 (cyclopropane-containing)
probe_reagents.observation: Ring-opened product 83 obtained under standard conditions, consistent with an alkyl radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: Control: reaction without L1
probe_reagents.observation: No reaction without L1; with L1, copper phenylacetylide + 3a gave product 39 in 80% yield, 96% ee, implicating the ligated Cu-acetylide as the reductant of alkyl halides

probe_reagents.name: Tertiary alkyl bromide 3b vs chloride 3c
probe_reagents.observation: Both gave the same enantioselectivity, arguing against outer-sphere/free SET stereochemistry differences

probe_reagents.name: L2CuI NMR binding study
probe_reagents.observation: 1H NMR of L2Cu(I) complex characterized binding mode of N,N,P-ligand to Cu(I)

notes: Enantiodiscrimination is rationalized by two pentacoordinated Cu(III) transition states: the re-TS is favored over si-TS due to steric clash between the (hetero)aryl group on substrate and the P-3,5-di-tert-butylphenyl group of the ligand, giving R-configured products. Authors note no solid experimental evidence yet for the stereochemical model and that DFT/further experiments are ongoing. Direct radical-Cu(II) coupling at quaternary centers is suppressed by steric hindrance, consistent with observed selectivity. Aggregated Cu(I)-acetylide (V) is proposed as an off-cycle resting form.
